confidence_rationale: Gumbel-Softmax directly addresses differentiable sampling of categorical variables, which is relevant to REE's first-action-class boundary. It is a later-option paper, not a reason to replace the current CEM path during the 563b repair. The immediate repair can be non-differentiable support instrumentation and minimal one-hot support preservation.
failure_signatures: If first-action class is treated only as an emergent argmax of a continuous decoder, CEM may never sample behaviourally meaningful categorical alternatives.
A differentiable categorical sampler would be premature if the live failure can be measured and repaired with default-off support guards.

mapping.source_claim_statement: Gumbel-Softmax provides a differentiable relaxation for categorical samples that can be annealed toward discrete categorical variables.
mapping.ree_translation: If REE later needs a trained categorical-first-action proposal stage, Gumbel-Softmax is a plausible implementation family. For V3-EXQ-563b, it should remain a design analogy: first measure whether the existing proposer can be made support-preserving without adding a new differentiable categorical path.
mapping.mapping_caveat: The paper is about stochastic computation graphs and discrete latent-variable learning, not CEM or MPC. It is relevant to categorical action sampling only as a later implementation pattern.
mapping.source_context: ICLR 2017 machine-learning paper on continuous relaxations for categorical variables and gradient-based training.

confidence_components.notes: Good anchor for future categorical-first proposal work; too indirect for the immediate support-preserving CEM patch.
